content: We can model the "anomaly score smoothness" of nodes in graph anomaly detection with a constrained sigmoid activation, where the asymptotic bounds of the sigmoid map naturally to the core observation that most graph nodes are normal and only a tiny minority are extreme anomalies. This framing lets us output calibrated, probability-aligned anomaly scores that naturally penalize overconfident outlier detections without requiring additional post-processing normalization of raw anomaly outputs.
